rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes when referring to component ports, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use helper declarations to align assumed types with actual types in comparisons, e.g., eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE annex blocks are syntactically valid and use visible symbols appropriately.
Model bounded behavior using range constraints on output variables.
Express guarantees as boolean conditions that constrain variable values.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use helper declarations to define boolean expressions for range checks.
Place AGREE contracts in appropriate AADL component scopes.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not mismatch types in helper declarations; align with expression types.
irrelevant_topics: AADL model fusion and component merging
